title: a few questions diffs between X0 vs XXX where to buy
display_title: a few questions diffs between X0 vs XXX where to buy
wikitext:
[]: a few questions (diffs between X.0 vs X.XX, where to buy)             Quote Post by dcelf » Thu Jul 18, 2013 6:03 am so i was just wondering whats the difference between EVA 1.0 and 1.11? 2.0 and 2.22? 3.0 and 3.33? is there a difference and how do i download/purchase them? sorry if its a stupid question, i just really wanna know. thanks

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jul 18, 2013 7:53 am 1.0, 2.0, and 3.0 are the theatrical versions of the first, second, and third "Rebuild" films. You can't buy them. You want to buy 1.11, 2.22, and 3.33 (when it comes out). These are the revised home video versions. You can buy them from most major retailers that carry anime. There is a version of the first film called 1.01, but it's an inferior version that you only want if you are a hardcore collector and you want to have every version in your collection. We've detailed differences between 1.01 and 1.11 in the wiki: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Guides:Evangelion_1.01_vs._Evangelion_1.11

[]:             Quote Post by Katana » Thu Jul 18, 2013 8:12 am The Eva Monkey wrote: There is a version of the first film called 1.01

[]:             Quote Post by dcelf » Wed Jul 24, 2013 8:59 pm thanks for the feedback, i just finished watching them and at the end of 2.22 it said "next time evangelion new theatrical edition Q" any idea what this means?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 24, 2013 9:21 pm Eva Q is 3.0/3.33. Q (originally titled "Kyu" from JoHaKyu narrative structure) is the Japanese title of the film, 3.0 is part of the International title. The Q in the title stands for "Quickening". Hope that helps!

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jul 24, 2013 10:53 pm I actually think the 1.01 version of "Jo" is neat. Certainly inferior, but neat. It preserves the original 1.85:1 aspect ratio rather than adjusting it to 1.77:1 (standard 16:9) like the 1.11 version does. (Though, I think the framing between 1.0 and 1.01 are different. I never saw the 1.0 version, so I can't say for sure.) But the 1.11 version has more scenes in it, better colors and picture quality, and is overall a better product. Heck, Anno even backs the camera up a little in the 1.11 version, showing wider shots than the 1.01 version, so you're not missing the stuff that was cropped by switching from the 1.85:1 aspect ratio to the 1.77:1 aspect ratio. If you had to pick one or the other, pick the 1.11 version. As for "Ha" and "Q," there's only the 2.22 and 3.33 versions of them available on DVD and Blu-ray. 2.0 goes a bit more chibi deformed from time to time than 2.22, from what I remember, as well as lacking in scenes that were brought in the subsequent 2.22 release. 3.33 just has better animation than 3.0 from what I've heard. But I haven't seen the 3.0 version, so I can't tell you one way or another.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed Jul 24, 2013 11:17 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: It preserves the original 1.85:1 aspect ratio rather than adjusting it to 1.77:1 (standard 16:9) like the 1.11 version does.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Jul 25, 2013 3:22 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: 3.33 just has better animation than 3.0 from what I've heard. But I haven't seen the 3.0 version, so I can't tell you one way or another.

[]:             Quote Post by dcelf » Thu Jul 25, 2013 6:48 am ok so what i gather about 3.33 by what i hear and the clips ive seen on youtube, is that its been released, but just not in america yet? cuz i cant find anywhere to buy it...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jul 25, 2013 7:15 am dcelf wrote: ok so what i gather about 3.33 by what i hear and the clips ive seen on youtube, is that its been released, but just not in america yet? cuz i cant find anywhere to buy it...

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Jul 25, 2013 7:32 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: What do you mean by "corrected left and right"?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jul 25, 2013 7:44 am Ah, I see. I thought you meant they had changed the direction in which the characters were facing.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Jul 25, 2013 8:15 am Unless you have some specific example of shots from 1.01 and 1.11 being rearranged in-frame to account for the almost negligible difference in aspect ratio I am going to be skeptical of your claim of Anno bothering with rearrangements between the two. Also note your claim is hampered by the fact that things were most likely fixed between 1.01 and 1.11 for the sake of animation errors and not reframing. Comparison to live action cinematography are also not valid because due to the nature of live action any material prepared ahead of shooting can not hope to be more than a guide. You're dealing with plenty of unpredictable and not fully controllable elements on the set. With animation you are 100% in control of every single pixel in every single frame all the time. Comparison to western animation and especially 3DGI is also off because those have different work flows. In Japan, once the layout is done it's pretty much exactly what you will get with the final film. There's lots more freedom in the digital animation age as far as composing the frame elements but the culture is still rooted strongly in the analog days where the instructions for the photography were still done by the animation director. Framing, panning and zooming is pretty much always there before it gets handed off to the animators who have to stick to it (because peg alignment was THE LAW). 1.01 is also definitely hampered by the terrible transfer. 1.00 was always a digital film, but due to the limitations of the time it had to be printed to film for distribution (Because James Cameron has yet to have threatened everyone into getting digital projectors). For some idiotic reason they decided to make the 1.01 DVD from the film instead of from the digital original. This has two problems: -When scanning film you will always get cropping because frame borders may not always align perfectly -SD Video, DVD and SD telecine are outdated and downright horrible processes that introduce their own artifacts including almost always additional bordering. So because of that 1.01 can never be considered as what was intended due to the layers of artifacts from needless processes involved. I only have one example of a 1.00/1.01/1.11 storyboard scanned but it's good enough for a demonstration: The layout. Drawn by Anno himself. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Newser » Thu Jul 25, 2013 8:43 am Do you happen to know what anime shows, as of today, have 2.35:1 aspect ratio?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jul 25, 2013 1:00 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Unless you have some specific example of shots from 1.01 and 1.11 being rearranged in-frame to account for the almost negligible difference in aspect ratio I am going to be skeptical of your claim of Anno bothering with rearrangements between the two. Also note your claim is hampered by the fact that things were most likely fixed between 1.01 and 1.11 for the sake of animation errors and not reframing.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Jul 25, 2013 2:49 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Well, for one thing, reanimation is not the same thing as reframing.

[]:             Quote Post by dcelf » Fri Jul 26, 2013 9:56 am when i first watched the original NGE series it was at a friends house and now i would like to buy them, and he wont sell it lol. i got 1.11 and 2.22 from the walmart website, but they dont have the series, so i looked around online some more(because nobody sells it locally) for the the 26 episode neon genesis evangelion DVD set and i cant find anywhere to buy them. can someone reccomend a site or a link to a website where i can buy it and have it shipped to me? im sorry this isnt technically a rebuild question, but i just really need to know. thanks
